page: Amazon Web Services g6.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, and 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU metrics, general memory bandwidth, and compression tasks fall within the average range. Notably, the large block memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% at 23.28 GB/sec. However, the integrated NVIDIA L4 GPU delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference, processing 11942.64 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a small 135M model and 53.77 tokens/sec for text generation on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g6.2xlarge offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring dedicated GPU acceleration without the overhead of larger multi-GPU instances. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC processing, local NVMe SSD storage, and Ada Lovelace architecture makes this instance highly suitable for small-to-medium LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, ray tracing, and database operations that benefit from GPU-accelerated compute and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier LLM inference capabilities, processing 2311.65 tokens/sec for medium 7B model prompts. Conversely, large block memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is highly suited for small-to-medium LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, and database operations requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU, 32 GB RAM, a 22 GB NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage for graphics and LLM inference workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA L4 GPU, local NVMe storage, and top-tier performance for small-to-medium LLM inference workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB DDR4 RAM
Equipped with one NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU featuring 22 GB VRAM
Includes 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier benchmark results for small and medium LLM inference
Best suited for graphics-intensive applications, database operations, and AI inference
